description_long: This object consists of two roughly hemispherical halves of a coconut shell, each displaying a hard, fibrous brown exterior and a white, edible inner layer of coconut flesh. The shells are irregularly broken, exposing the rough, woody interior and the smooth, slightly glossy white coconut meat. The outer surface is textured with a dense, linear pattern of brown fibers, giving it a rugged, natural appearance. The interior of one half is almost entirely hollow, while the other retains a thicker layer of white flesh. There are no visible stem scars or attachment points on the shells. The object is shown isolated on a plain background, with no contextual clues about its environment.
description: Two broken halves of a brown, fibrous coconut shell with white flesh inside, showing rough exteriors and smooth interiors. The shells are empty and irregularly split, revealing both the woody and edible layers.
description_view_0: View 0 shows both coconut halves with the white flesh clearly visible in one half and the rough brown shell exterior prominent on the other.
description_view_1: View 1 highlights the interior of both halves, with one showing a thick white layer of flesh and the other mostly hollow and brown.
description_view_2: View 2 presents the broken edges and the contrast between the white flesh and the fibrous brown shell, with both interiors visible.
description_view_3: View 3 emphasizes the rough, fibrous exterior of both halves and the hollowed-out interior of one shell.
attribute_husk_pattern: The outer husk displays a dense, linear arrangement of coarse brown fibers running mostly parallel along the shell's curvature, with some irregularities and small patches of lighter or darker brown. The texture is rugged and uneven, with visible striations and occasional small pits or marks.
